description_long: This barn features a large, asymmetrical roof with multiple intersecting sections, giving it a distinctive and somewhat modern appearance. The main structure is supported by simple, straight posts, and there is an open, covered area adjacent to the main enclosed space, likely used for storage or sheltering equipment. The roof is gently sloped and extends over both the main building and the attached lean-to, providing ample coverage. The exterior walls are plain and unadorned, with minimal visible detailing or ornamentation. A single, prominent entryway is visible, with a simple rectangular opening and a small overhang above it. There are no visible windows, giving the barn a utilitarian and functional look.
description: A modern, asymmetrical barn with a large, intersecting sloped roof and a simple, open-sided lean-to. The structure is plain, with a single main entry and no visible windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entryway, the intersecting rooflines, and the open-sided lean-to supported by posts.
description_view_1: Rear angled view highlighting the extended roof and the open lean-to area, with the main entryway partially visible.
description_view_2: Back view displaying the long, continuous roofline and the plain, windowless rear wall.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizing the depth of the structure, the open lean-to, and the main entryway under the roof overhang.
attribute_roof_type: asymmetrical intersecting gable roof with a lean-to extension
attribute_siding_pattern: plain, flat panels with no visible decorative pattern
attribute_door_style: single, large rectangular hinged door with a simple overhang
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows on any exterior wall
